reason: Scalar division produces a single value with no collection; stable-equal-element ordering has nothing to order.
Parallel uploads, interrupted transfers, and partial files are real concurrency defects for file upload.
Price-range boundary comparisons can hit float precision (e.g. 19.99 vs 20.00), affecting which items match.
Rows with equal sort keys need a specified, stable order for reproducible CSV exports.
Rounding yields a single scalar per share; there is no collection whose equal-element order could vary.
Scalar division has no spatial/boundary touching; the real edge is divide-by-zero (empty), not adjacency.
Half-way values (e.g. .005) are exactly the tie boundary: round-half-up vs round-half-even is a real defect.
Exporting while records change or concurrent exports can produce an inconsistent snapshot.
Values exactly at price-range edges raise inclusive/exclusive boundary questions (does max match?).
Items exactly at a page boundary or a count exactly divisible by twenty are real off-by-one/empty-last-page edges.
Text search is about match semantics (substring/exact), not equal-things-touching/merging; adjacency is over-reach here.
Null, NaN, or absent share input to the rounding step is a plausible empty/single-value defect.
